core_mechanism: the Stokes phenomenon in semiclassical/WKB asymptotics: exponentially subdominant terms switch on discontinuously across Stokes lines in the complex plane of the evolution variable, so locally-valid asymptotic solutions must be connected globally, and interference between contributions from multiple complex turning points yields an oscillatory amplitude
brief_reason: The abstract states that WKB solutions are only local while the production rate requires global information, and that incorporating the Stokes phenomenon quantitatively explains the oscillatory (and phase-shifted) spectrum arising from sub-structure in the background.
